questions.question: If $2^x = 32$, what is the value of $x$?

A group of hikers is planning a trip to a remote area. They estimate that their fuel consumption will increase by a factor of $3^2$ due to the rough terrain. If their initial fuel consumption was 4 gallons per hour, what will be their new fuel consumption?

The population of a certain animal species is growing exponentially according to the equation $P(t) = 2^t cdot P_0$, where $P_0$ is the initial population. If the population doubles every 2 years, what is the value of $t$ when the population reaches 4 times its initial value?

A park ranger estimates that the number of visitors to a park will increase by a factor of $5^2$ over the next 5 years. If the current number of visitors is 100, what will be the estimated number of visitors in 5 years?
If $3^x = 81$, what is the value of $x$?

A group of friends plan to go on a road trip. They estimate that their fuel consumption will decrease by a factor of $2^2$ if they drive at a constant speed of 60 mph. If their initial fuel consumption was 6 gallons per hour, what will be their new fuel consumption?

A certain species of tree is growing in a forest. The number of trees is increasing exponentially according to the equation $N(t) = 3^t cdot N_0$, where $N_0$ is the initial number of trees. If the number of trees doubles every 3 years, what is the value of $t$ when the number of trees reaches 8 times its initial value? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A traveler is planning a trip to a remote area. She estimates that her fuel consumption will increase by a factor of $2^x$ due to the rough terrain. If her initial fuel consumption was 8 gallons per hour and she wants to know her new fuel consumption when $x = 3$, what will be her new fuel consumption? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of understanding exponential growth and decay in real-world contexts.
Encourage students to use mathematical models to solve problems and make predictions.
Remind students to show their work and explain their reasoning when solving problems.
